stories.headline: Israel, Lebanon Agree to 10-Day Ceasefire
stories.summary:
Trump says Israel and Lebanon agreed to a 10-day ceasefire. Netanyahu confirmed the truce as a step toward broader peace, with US officials working on a lasting deal.

 (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Iran war live: Ceasefire starts in Lebanon as Trump says Tehran deal close
stories.summary: Death toll from Israeli attacks on Lebanon reached 2,196 on Thursday as 10-day ceasefire announced.

stories.headline: Celebrations in Lebanon as 10-day ceasefire with Israel begins
stories.summary: Celebratory gunfire heard in Beirut as truce takes effect, though Lebanese military reports violations.

stories.headline: ‘Trump forced Israel into a ceasefire’ with Lebanon

stories.headline: US-brokered 10-day ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon goes into effect

stories.headline: Australia's most decorated living soldier granted bail over war crime charges
stories.summary: Lawyers argued that Ben Roberts-Smith wouldn't be able to defend himself properly from prison.

stories.headline: Trump Says Iran’s Concessions Pave Way for Deal to End War
stories.summary: President Donald Trump claimed Iran has made key concessions in negotiations with the US and that a deal to end the war — which has left thousands dead and rattled energy markets — could be announced “fairly soon.”

stories.summary: US President Donald Trump has described the war on Iran which has killed more than 2,000 people as a ‘little diversion’.

stories.headline: ‘Vengeance for all’: How Iran’s Lego videos won narrative war against Trump

stories.headline: BofA Sees 'Structural Drivers' Working Against Dollar
stories.summary: Kamal Sharma, G-10 FX strategy director at Bank of America, discusses the dollar's status as a safe haven as some banks are declaring the war-driven haven rally is likely over. He speaks on Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Acting ICE Director Says He Plans to Resign in May
stories.summary: Todd Lyons said he would leave to spend more time with his family. He has spoken about a surge in threats against ICE officers, saying that he knew the reality firsthand.

What we know on day 1,513The EU expects to start releasing a new €90bn loan to Ukraine in the second quarter, the bloc’s economy chief told AFP on Thursday. stories.headline: Sudan’s Prime Minister: This is the path out of the horrors of war
stories.summary: Kamil Idris outlines his government’s vision for ending the war, rebuilding the country and restoring civilian rule.

stories.headline: Why are anti-war protests in the West muted on Iran?
stories.summary: Despite global opposition, Iran war protests lag behind Gaza and Ukraine, shaped by fatigue, fear and disillusionment.

stories.headline: Watch: Sunday elections — Bulgaria's fight for stable government?
stories.summary: Bulgaria heads to the polls this Sunday for its eighth parliamentary election in five years. The country is the poorest in the EU according to GDP per capita, and the electorate is exhausted by a prolonged political crisis. So, who is stepping in to fill the void?

stories.headline: Cameroon sees mass turnout for Pope Leo XIV visit as he calls for peace
stories.summary: Cameroon saw thousands of faithful gather on Wednesday 16 April at Bamenda’s airfield, where Pope Leo XIV celebrated an open‑air Mass and called for a “decisive change of course” towards peace.

stories.headline: Trump’s ‘fossil fuelled war’ is the perfect opportunity to end reliance on oil and gas, experts say

stories.headline: Finance ministers and top bankers raise serious concerns about Mythos AI model
stories.summary: Experts say Mythos potentially has an unprecedented ability to identify and exploit cybersecurity weaknesses.

stories.headline: Artemis commander tells BBC about 'powerful' moment crater named after his late wife

stories.headline: Odd Lots: Planet Money Explains the World (Podcast)
stories.summary: There are a lot of things to be annoyed about in modern life. The high cost of food and housing and childcare. Dating apps that don’t seem to work. The fear of AI replacing you at your job. These are all common complaints and concerns, and each of them can be traced to a specific economic phenomenon or market structure issue. Once you start thinking about the world in this way, you can’t unsee it. In this episode, we speak with Planet Money co-ho

stories.headline: Goldman Sachs Says Market Recovery Hinges on 'Rates Relief'
stories.summary: Christian Mueller-Glissmann, head of asset allocation research at Goldman Sachs, discusses the equities rally, the impact of US-Iran de-escalation on sentiment, and whether the markets recovery is sustainable. "I think we need central banks to shift back to a bit to where we were before," Mueller-Glissmann tells Bloomberg Television. "We need the rates relief to come in." (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Trump-Linked Investors Want to Pour $1.8 Billion Into Bosnia
stories.summary: Two figures who worked on President Donald Trump’s attempt to overturn the results of the 2020 election are pursuing a major infrastructure project in Bosnia.

stories.headline: JPMorgan, UBS See Little Stock Upside Left in Europe This Year
stories.summary: Looming downgrades to over-optimistic earnings expectations mean European stocks will struggle to rise further this year, according to strategists surveyed by Bloomberg.

stories.headline: India’s Rupee Is Asia’s Biggest Gainer on Oil FX Window Report
stories.summary: The Indian rupee led gains in Asia after a report that the central bank has asked state-owned oil refiners to route their dollar purchases through a special credit facility.

stories.headline: Global Banks Scramble for Land in Mumbai
stories.summary:
Mumbai’s skyline is changing as Wall Street firms pile in to tap India’s financial boom. But in a land-starved city, that growth is colliding with communities and intensifying the fight for space.

 (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: House Votes to Extend Expiring FISA Surveillance Law for 10 Days
stories.summary: The Senate would need to also approve the stopgap measure that passed the House early Friday. Libertarian-leaning House Republicans had balked at a long-term extension.

stories.summary: Thieves believed to have escaped into sewers after holding staff and customers in Crédit Agricole branch for two hoursArmed robbers held 25 people hostage at a bank in Naples for two hours on Thursday, before fleeing through a tunnel.The three thieves entered a branch of Crédit Agricole in the southern Italian city at about 11.30am, taking hostage staff and customers, who were freed by police a couple of hours later. stories.summary: The aid is roughly equivalent to a year's worth of crude oil imports by Asean countries.

stories.headline: House Votes to Preserve Deportation Protections for Haitians, Rebuking Trump
stories.summary: The action was largely symbolic since the president would be all but certain to veto the bill, but the bipartisan vote reflected resistance within his own ranks on his signature issue.

stories.headline: Judge Again Halts Aboveground Construction on Trump Ballroom
stories.summary: A federal judge wrote that an exception he made for work on security features did not cover most of the construction on the larger ballroom project Trump has proposed.

stories.headline: Federal judge sets new limits on Trump ballroom construction
stories.summary: U.S. District Judge Richard Leon clarified that some national security work can continue while the overall project is halted. Trump attacked him on social media.

stories.headline: House votes to restore protections for Haitians, defying Trump
stories.summary: Ten House Republicans joined Democrats to oppose President Donald Trump on his immigration policy Thursday, voting to restore temporary protections for Haitians living in the United States.

stories.headline: South African opposition figure Malema sentenced to five years in prison for firing a gun
stories.summary: Malema is appealing against the decision, which means he is not going to prison immediately.

stories.headline: India pushes for women's quota bill as row over parliamentary seats intensifies
stories.summary: Southern Indian leaders urge mass mobilisation over concerns about redrawing electoral boundaries.

stories.headline: Peru election drags into second day after ballot delivery fiasco
